scope: sitewide header footer breadcrumbs navigation plan
sourceGuidance: LMHG plugin/content-design guidance for primary navigation, footer groups, service-area exclusions, and graph-backed breadcrumbs.
Gutenberg template planner rules: templates/*.html, flat parts/*.html, core Navigation/template-part blocks first, no PHP in block-template HTML.
guardrails: Planning only; no WordPress menu, page, template, metadata, or visible surface was changed.
Header and footer must be implemented as Gutenberg block-theme template parts: parts/header.html and parts/footer.html.
Use core Navigation, Group, Columns, Buttons, Heading, and Paragraph blocks before custom code.
Do not import Astro code, Starwind markup, old CSS, legacy templates, old block markup, or prior plugin implementation code.
Footer city/service-area links must exclude Bardstown, Clarksville, Jeffersonville, and New Albany unless user explicitly approves future pages.
Breadcrumbs must follow approved relationship data, not raw URL parsing.

breadcrumbs.implementationTarget: Gutenberg-native breadcrumb pattern in templates/page content; no Astro Breadcrumb component or URL-parser runtime.
coreBlocks: core/html only if semantic nav attributes are explicitly approved later
pureGutenbergPlan: Render visible breadcrumb trails as normal Gutenberg block markup generated from the approved relationship graph.
Keep breadcrumb labels human-readable and separate from SEO title tags.
Use Home as the first breadcrumb, then hub/core parent, then current page.
Do not infer breadcrumbs from URL segments alone.
Do not import Astro Breadcrumb.astro, Starwind breadcrumb components, or route-parser packages.
breadcrumbs.schemaNote: Visible breadcrumbs can be 100% Gutenberg block markup. BreadcrumbList JSON-LD should be handled by approved SEO/schema tooling later if needed; do not smuggle custom PHP into block-template HTML.

openDecisions: Confirm whether Specialties should remain a top-level link only or expose a separate dropdown in addition to the Services nested menu.
Confirm whether FAQ should remain a top-level link only or expose About LMHG, Our Approach, and Cost as a dropdown.
Confirm whether HIPAA should link to /compliance/ or remain pending until a separate HIPAA destination exists.
Confirm whether the Client Portal footer item should stay as # until an external portal URL is supplied.
